clinicalProblem.whenToUse: When BP remains uncontrolled despite multiple antihypertensives or requires 4+ medications
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: Confirm resistance, exclude pseudoresistance, escalate pharmacological treatment, or refer to specialist

navigationPath.purpose: Entry point for treatment resistance evaluation
Check BP thresholds and medication count

ckdStage.validation: Stage 4 or greater impacts diuretic choice

validationRules: Resistance confirmed if: office BP ≥130/80 on ≥3 antihypertensives
Resistance confirmed if: office BP <130/80 but requires ≥4 antihypertensives
Preferred combination: ACEi or ARB + CCB + thiazide-like diuretics

rules: Check if BP ≥130/80 on ≥3 meds → confirmed resistance
Check if BP <130/80 on ≥4 meds → confirmed resistance
If confirmed, exclude pseudoresistance through 6-step process
Step 1: Ensure accurate office BP measurements
Step 3: Obtain home/work/ambulatory BP to exclude white-coat effect
Step 4: Identify and reverse contributing lifestyle factors
Step 5: Discontinue or minimize interfering substances
Step 6: Screen for secondary causes of hypertension
If pseudoresistance excluded → escalate pharmacological treatment
Replace thiazide with chlorthalidone or indapamide
Add spironolactone or eplerenone if eGFR ≥45
Use chlorthalidone or loop diuretics for CKD stage 4+
Add agents with different MOA based on heart rate
Add BB/central sympatholytics/nondihydropyridine CCB for elevated HR
Add dual ERA or potent vasodilators as last-line
Refer to specialist if secondary cause suspected or BP uncontrolled >6 months

categories.name: Elevated Heart Rate
categories.add: BB, central sympatholytics, or nondihydropyridine CCB

thresholds.sbp: N/A - treatment resistance is chronic management, not acute crisis

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Follow stepwise pharmacological escalation; consider specialist referral if refractory
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Reassess in 1-3 months after medication changes; if uncontrolled >6 months, refer
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Confirm with ambulatory BP monitoring after each major medication change
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Flag patients on ≥4 antihypertensives or requiring dual ERA/vasodilators for care team review
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log resistance confirmation, pseudoresistance exclusions completed, medication regimen, and referral status
